title: ShellFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing shell features in a component and supports the ability to create new shell features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a ShellFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the shell you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ShellFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified shell feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified shell feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of shell features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Shell Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new shell feature.

add.title: ShellFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created ShellFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A ShellFeatureInput object that defines the desired shell. Use the createInput method to create a new ShellFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the ShellFeatureInput object) to define the shell.

related_samples.name: Shell Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new shell feature.

classType.title: ShellFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: ShellFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a ShellFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the shell you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the ShellFeatureInput object.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = shellFeatures_var.createInput(inputEntities)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = shellFeatures_var.createInput(inputEntities, isTangentChain)

createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created ShellFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: inputEntities
parameters.description: The collection contains the faces to remove and the bodies to perform shell. Fails if any faces are input, and the owning bodies of the faces are also input.

parameters.name: isTangentChain
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not faces that are tangentially connected to the input faces (if any) will also be included. It defaults to true.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

related_samples.name: Shell Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new shell feature.

item.title: ShellFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified shell feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ShellFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified shell feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: ShellFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of shell features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = shellFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: ShellFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = shellFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: ShellFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = shellFeatures_var.objectType
